metadata.title: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
metadata.study_design: Intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate during prolonged overnight fast (12-19h) in healthy, lean young men and women
metadata.demographics: Healthy, lean young men and women (n=12), matched for age and BMI, free from disease, weight stable, not taking lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: British Journal of Nutrition (2011), 105, 857–865
Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
Kyriakoula Marinou1,2, Martin Adiels3, Leanne Hodson1, Keith N. Frayn1, Fredrik Karpe1 and Barbara A. Fielding1*
Volunteers were studied during a prolonged overnight fast (12–19h) using an intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate.
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied, and matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied

methods_participants.label: Inclusion Criteria - Health Status
methods_participants.value: Healthy, lean, weight stable, free from any disease, not taking lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism

evidence.quote: Subjects, recruited from the wider Oxford community via advertisement, were free from any disease, weight stable and not taking any lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism
The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle

methods_participants.label: Pre-study preparation - exercise and alcohol restriction

evidence.quote: Before the study day, subjects were asked to avoid foodstuffs naturally enriched in 13C for 48h

methods_participants.label: Body Composition Measurement
methods_participants.method: Gas chromatography and gas chromatography-mass spectrometry

evidence.quote: Fat mass was measured by bioelectrical impedance analysis, and fat-free mass was calculated as the difference between total body weight and fat mass
At time 0, a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate
plasma NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations were determined enzymatically

methods_participants.label: VLDL isolation method
methods_participants.value: Sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation
methods_participants.method: sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: VLDL (Svedberg flotation rate (Sf) 60–400) and VLDL (Sf 20–60) were isolated by sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation in an SW40Ti swinging bucket rotor
After 12h fasting, the men had a less advantageous metabolic profile

methods_participants.label: Participant matching criteria
methods_participants.value: matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference

evidence.quote: matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference

methods_participants.label: Blood sampling - baseline
methods_participants.value: Baseline blood sample for background isotopic enrichment

evidence.quote: A cannula was inserted into an antecubital vein, and a baseline blood sample was taken for background isotopic enrichment

narrative_results.statement: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

narrative_results.statement: After 12h fasting, women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose and TAG but higher plasma NEFA concentrations

evidence.quote: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P,0·05) and TAG (P,0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P,0·05) concentrations

narrative_results.statement: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production, but in men, this was not observed

evidence.quote: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P,0·01)

narrative_results.statement: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-TAG

evidence.quote: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG.

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 describes kinetic plateau timing differences between sexes; Pass 2 describes production rate differences. These are related but distinct mechanistic findings that cannot both be the primary focus of the same statement.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: The ability of young women to partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG may contribute to their more advantageous metabolic profile compared with young men.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P,0·05) and TAG (P,0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P,0·05) concentrations
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P<0·05) and TAG (P<0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P<0·05) concentrations.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: the transfer of 13C from [U-13C]palmitate to plasma [13C]3-OHB reached a plateau 6–7h after the start of the infusion in women but was still increasing at 6h in men
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: In women, the high isotopic enrichment of plasma 3-OHB suggested that systemic plasma fatty acids were the major source of 3-OHB production. However, in men, this was not observed during the course of the study (P<0·01).

structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-1 or VLDL-2-TAG.

structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL2).

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL1 or VLDL2-TAG

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL 1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL 2)
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: The present findings suggest that in young lean women, a higher plasma concentration of 3-OHB, compared with men, is due to a greater production

semantic_groups.rationale: Pass 1 specifies exact numbers (6 men and 6 women), Pass 2 only mentions presence of both sexes. Same underlying fact but different level of detail.
Pass 1 includes 'lean' descriptor which Pass 2 omits, but both capture the same core inclusion criteria. Negligible difference.
Both describe follicular phase timing. Pass 2 adds clarification 'first week' but conveys the same fact.
Both specify 48 hours food avoidance of 13C-enriched foods. Only difference is 'hours' vs 'h' abbreviation.
Both specify 24 hours restriction from strenuous exercise and alcohol. Minor wording variation ('restriction' vs 'avoidance') and abbreviation difference.
Both describe low-fat meal requirement. Pass 1 specifies 'evening meal' context while Pass 2 generalizes to 'meal requirement', but same fact.
Both specify overnight fasting. Minor wording difference in framing ('fasting status' vs 'fasting duration').
Both describe bioelectrical impedance analysis for body composition. Pass 2 adds methodological detail about fat-free mass calculation, complementary information.
Both describe the same isotope infusion dose (0.04 mmol/kg per min) via continuous IV infusion with identical units
Pass 1 provides complete tracer identification and purity; Pass 2 only reports purity value. Same underlying fact but different completeness
Both report 7 hours infusion duration; minor unit notation difference (hours vs h) is negligible
Both describe enzymatic determination method; Pass 2 adds specific metabolites measured (NEFA, VLDL-TAG, 3-OHB) which is complementary detail
Both describe RIA/radioimmunoassay for plasma insulin measurement; identical information with minor wording variation
Both report N=10 for VLDL isolation; Pass 2 adds method detail not present in Pass 1
Both describe sequential flotation via density gradient ultracentrifugation; Pass 2 adds specific rotor and conditions (SW40Ti, 40000rpm, 15°C)
Both identify GC and GC-MS as methods; Pass 2 adds sample preparation detail (FAME from NEFA and VLDL-TAG fractions)
Pass 1 contains baseline fasting duration information; Pass 2 is empty
Both describe the same fact: 12 participants in the study population, using different terminology
Pass 2 contains participant matching criteria; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 2 contains isotope tracer infusion details; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 2 contains blood sampling baseline procedure; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 2 contains VLDL isolation S 60-400 duration information; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 2 contains VLDL isolation S 20-60 duration information; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 2 contains 3-OHB isotopic enrichment assay sample volume; Pass 1 is empty
Pass 1 is empty; Pass 2 contains a measurement specification for 3-OHB standard curve at 100 mmol/l. No comparison possible.
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions

Pass 1 describes kinetic plateau timing differences between sexes; Pass 2 describes production rate differences. These are related but distinct mechanistic findings that cannot both be the primary focus of the same statement.
Identical text across both extractions
Same fact with minor formatting differences: 'VLDL1 or VLDL2' vs 'VLDL-1 or VLDL-2' and 'VLDL2-TAG' vs 'VLDL-2 TAG'
Same fact with negligible wording difference: 'in the plasma' vs 'in plasma'
Identical text across both extractions
